content: One Health is a collaborative, multisectoral approach recognizing that human health is interconnected with animal and environmental health. The framework addresses complex health challenges at the human-animal-environment interface: zoonotic diseases (60% of human pathogens), antimicrobial resistance (driven by use in humans, animals, agriculture), food safety (contamination along farm-to-table chain), and climate change impacts on disease patterns. One Health emphasizes that solutions require coordination across human medicine, veterinary medicine, environmental science, and related disciplines. Approximately 75% of emerging infectious diseases are zoonotic, making One Health critical for pandemic prevention.
source: WHO-FAO-OIE-WHOE One Health Joint Plan, CDC One Health framework
